mnemonics.title: Fragmentation Types
mnemonics.content: Think 'INside' for Internal (wasted INSIDE partition), 'OUTside' for External (scattered OUTside)

mnemonics.title: Memory Allocation Algorithms
mnemonics.content: F-B-W-N: First Find Best Way aNytime - helps remember First Fit, Best Fit, Worst Fit, Next Fit

mnemonics.title: Compaction Purpose
mnemonics.content: Compaction = COMPressed holes into one block - 'COM' for COMbine

acronyms.expansion: Process Control Block - stores process memory information including base/limit
acronyms.usage: Used by OS to track each process's memory boundaries

acronyms.expansion: Random Access Memory - the primary memory where processes are loaded in contiguous allocation
acronyms.usage: Memory that gets partitioned in contiguous allocation schemes

visualTips.visualization: Think of memory as a long hallway (100 meters). Each room (process) occupies consecutive rooms. Empty rooms between occupied ones are 'holes'. Compaction removes walls between empty rooms to create one big empty room.

Internal: You reserved a 10-seat table for 6 people - 4 seats wasted INSIDE. External: 10 small tables scattered around a restaurant, none can seat a group of 8 together - can't use the space effectively.
Like a student's desk (base = desk number) with a boundary marker (limit = number of desks they can use). Teacher ensures student stays within their assigned desks.

analogies.analogy: Like assigning a family to consecutive hotel rooms rather than separate rooms scattered across the building - they stay together in one block.
Like finding the smallest parking space that fits your car - leaves minimal empty space but takes longer to find.
Like always giving the largest room to the smallest group, hoping the remaining large space will be useful for future large groups.
Like defragmenting a hard drive - moving all scattered free files together to create one large contiguous free space.
Like having small coins scattered in different pockets - you have money but can't buy something that costs more than any single coin.
